items.original_title: `DimensionX` functions should use `element.offsetX` instead of CSS value
items.original_body:
## Explanation
Currently, the `DimensionWidth` and `DimensionHeight` WebElement functions use the `element.style.height` and `element.style.width`. 

However, the actual width of an element can differ from the height and width defined in the CSS, depending on the element's `display`, `min-width`, `max-width`, `overflow` and `transform` CSS properties (among others), and also depending on the element's content, its parent node and the browser's current resolution. The same goes for the height.

## How to fix it
Instead of checking the value of `element.style.width` and `element.style.height`, the value of [`element.offsetWidth`](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/HTMLElement/offsetWidth) and [`element.offsetHeight`](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/HTMLElement/offsetHeight) should be used. These represent the actual width and height of the element as it is currently rendered on the page.

Depending on the desired usage, [`element.scrollWidth`](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-us/docs/Web/API/Element/scrollWidth) and [`element.scrollHeight`](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-us/docs/Web/API/Element/scrollHeight) might also be used, although I would expect this to have a function of its own.
items.mutated_title: Instead of checking the value of `element.style.width` and `element.style.height`, the value of `element.offsetWidth` and `element.offsetHeight` should be used when invoking the API.
items.mutated_body:
## Explanation
Currently, the `DimensionWidth` and `DimensionHeight` WebElement functions use the `element.style.height` and `element.style.width`. 

However, the actual width of an element can differ from the height and width defined in the CSS, depending on the element's `display`, `min-width`, `max-width`, `overflow` and `transform` CSS properties (among others), and also depending on the element's content, its parent node and the browser's current resolution. The same goes for the height.

## How to fix it
Instead of checking the value of `element.style.width` and `element.style.height`, the value of [`element.offsetWidth`](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/HTMLElement/offsetWidth) and [`element.offsetHeight`](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/HTMLElement/offsetHeight) should be used. These represent the actual width and height of the element as it is currently rendered on the page.

Depending on the desired usage, [`element.scrollWidth`](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-us/docs/Web/API/Element/scrollWidth) and [`element.scrollHeight`](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-us/docs/Web/API/Element/scrollHeight) might also be used, although I would expect this to have a function of its own.

items.original_title: Kibana communication issue with Elasticsearch "Kibana server is not ready yet"
items.original_body:
<!---
1. Verify first that your issue/request is not already reported on GitHub.

2. Verify that your question is not covered in the docs: https://devilbox.readthedocs.io

3. PLEASE FILL OUT ALL REQUIRED INFORMATION BELOW! Otherwise it might take more time to properly handle this bug report.
-->


### ISSUE TYPE
<!-- DO NOT CHANGE THIS -->
 - Bug Report
<!-- DO NOT CHANGE THIS -->


### Checklist
<!-- ENSURE YOU HAVE DONE THE FOLLOWING -->
* [x] `.env` file is attached
* [x] `./check-config.sh` output is added below
* [x] `docker-compose logs` output is added below
* [x] `docker-compose.override.yml` is attached (if exists)
* [ ] Custom configs from `cfg/` dir are attached (if exist)
* [x] I've looked through the docs: https://devilbox.readthedocs.io/en/latest/
* [x] I've looked through existing issues: https://github.com/cytopia/devilbox/issues
* [x] I've read troubleshooting: https://devilbox.readthedocs.io/en/latest/support/troubleshooting.html


### OS / ENVIRONMENT
<!-- COMPLETE ALL 6 BULLET POINTS BELOW: -->
1. Host operating system and version: Windows 10
2. (Windows only) Native Docker or Docker Toolbox: 
3. Docker version: 3.3.3 (64133)
4. Docker Compose version: 1.29.1
5. (Linux) Is SELinux enabled?:
6. What git commit hash are you on?: cdfb55ec2d061bb20bb6aad1792034d995a855a2 (HEAD -> master, origin/master, origin/HEAD)


### SUMMARY
Trying to view Kibana from the browser (http://localhost:5601/) shows the message, "Kibana server is not ready yet"


### STEPS TO REPRODUCE
After getting the Kibana error after running the containers, I tried removing and re-creating the containers. Having the same result, I tried upgrading the containers. This also had the same result.

### EXPECTED BEHAVIOUR
Opening http://localhost:5601 should show the Kibana web app.


### ACTUAL BEHAVIOUR
The "Kibana server is not ready yet" text is displayed in the browser instead.


### OTHER INFORMATION

#### Start command
<!-- Add the command you have used to start the devilbox -->
```
docker-compose stop
docker-compose kill
docker-compose ps // nothing running
docker-compose rm -f
docker-compose pull
docker-compose up
```

#### Outputs
.env

docker-compose.override.yml
```yml
# vim: set ft=yaml:
# https://www.docker.elastic.co
---
version: '2.3'

services:

 # -----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
 # ELK Stack
 # -----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
 elastic:
 image: docker.elastic.co/elasticsearch/elasticsearch-oss:${ELK_SERVER:-6.6.1}
 hostname: elastic
 ports:
 - "${LOCAL_LISTEN_ADDR}${HOST_PORT_ELK_ELASTIC:-9200}:9200"
 networks:
 app_net:
 ipv4_address: 172.16.238.240
 environment:
 - TZ=${TIMEZONE:-UTC}
 - discovery.type=single-node
 volumes:
 - devilbox-elastic:/usr/share/elasticsearch/data

 logstash:
 image: docker.elastic.co/logstash/logstash-oss:${ELK_SERVER:-6.6.1}
 hostname: logstash
 ports:
 - "${LOCAL_LISTEN_ADDR}${HOST_PORT_ELK_LOGSTASH:-9600}:9600"
 networks:
 app_net:
 ipv4_address: 172.16.238.241
 environment:
 - TZ=${TIMEZONE:-UTC}
 - LOG_LEVEL=info
 - HTTP_HOST=0.0.0.0
 - HTTP_PORT=9600
 - CONFIG_RELOAD_AUTOMATIC=true
 - CONFIG_RELOAD_INTERVAL=300
 - DB_HOST=DATABASE-SERVER
 volumes:
 - devilbox-logstash:/var/lib/logstash
 depends_on:
 - elastic

 kibana:
 image: docker.elastic.co/kibana/kibana-oss:${ELK_SERVER:-6.6.1}
 hostname: kibana
 ports:
 - "${LOCAL_LISTEN_ADDR}${HOST_PORT_ELK_KIBANA:-5601}:5601"
 networks:
 app_net:
 ipv4_address: 172.16.238.242
 environment:
 - TZ=${TIMEZONE:-UTC}
 - ELASTICSEARCH_URL=http://elastic:9200
 depends_on:
 - elastic

volumes:
 devilbox-elastic:
 devilbox-logstash:

```
items.mutated_title: It was boring and lacking excitement
items.mutated_body:
<!---
1. Verify first that your issue/request is not already reported on GitHub.

2. Verify that your question is not covered in the docs: https://devilbox.readthedocs.io

3. PLEASE FILL OUT ALL REQUIRED INFORMATION BELOW! Otherwise it might take more time to properly handle this bug report.
-->


### ISSUE TYPE
<!-- DO NOT CHANGE THIS -->
 - Bug Report
<!-- DO NOT CHANGE THIS -->


### Checklist
<!-- ENSURE YOU HAVE DONE THE FOLLOWING -->
* [x] `.env` file is attached
* [x] `./check-config.sh` output is added below
* [x] `docker-compose logs` output is added below
* [x] `docker-compose.override.yml` is attached (if exists)
* [ ] Custom configs from `cfg/` dir are attached (if exist)
* [x] I've looked through the docs: https://devilbox.readthedocs.io/en/latest/
* [x] I've looked through existing issues: https://github.com/cytopia/devilbox/issues
* [x] I've read troubleshooting: https://devilbox.readthedocs.io/en/latest/support/troubleshooting.html


### OS / ENVIRONMENT
<!-- COMPLETE ALL 6 BULLET POINTS BELOW: -->
1. Host operating system and version: Windows 10
2. (Windows only) Native Docker or Docker Toolbox: 
3. Docker version: 3.3.3 (64133)
4. Docker Compose version: 1.29.1
5. (Linux) Is SELinux enabled?:
6. What git commit hash are you on?: cdfb55ec2d061bb20bb6aad1792034d995a855a2 (HEAD -> master, origin/master, origin/HEAD)


### SUMMARY
Trying to view Kibana from the browser (http://localhost:5601/) shows the message, "Kibana server is not ready yet"


### STEPS TO REPRODUCE
After getting the Kibana error after running the containers, I tried removing and re-creating the containers. Having the same result, I tried upgrading the containers. This also had the same result.

### EXPECTED BEHAVIOUR
Opening http://localhost:5601 should show the Kibana web app.


### ACTUAL BEHAVIOUR
The "Kibana server is not ready yet" text is displayed in the browser instead.


### OTHER INFORMATION

#### Start command
<!-- Add the command you have used to start the devilbox -->
```
docker-compose stop
docker-compose kill
docker-compose ps // nothing running
docker-compose rm -f
docker-compose pull
docker-compose up
```

docker-compose.override.yml
```yml
# vim: set ft=yaml:
# https://www.docker.elastic.co
---
version: '2.3'

services:

 # -----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
 # ELK Stack
 # -----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
 elastic:
 image: docker.elastic.co/elasticsearch/elasticsearch-oss:${ELK_SERVER:-6.6.1}
 hostname: elastic
 ports:
 - "${LOCAL_LISTEN_ADDR}${HOST_PORT_ELK_ELASTIC:-9200}:9200"
 networks:
 app_net:
 ipv4_address: 172.16.238.240
 environment:
 - TZ=${TIMEZONE:-UTC}
 - discovery.type=single-node
 volumes:
 - devilbox-elastic:/usr/share/elasticsearch/data

 logstash:
 image: docker.elastic.co/logstash/logstash-oss:${ELK_SERVER:-6.6.1}
 hostname: logstash
 ports:
 - "${LOCAL_LISTEN_ADDR}${HOST_PORT_ELK_LOGSTASH:-9600}:9600"
 networks:
 app_net:
 ipv4_address: 172.16.238.241
 environment:
 - TZ=${TIMEZONE:-UTC}
 - LOG_LEVEL=info
 - HTTP_HOST=0.0.0.0
 - HTTP_PORT=9600
 - CONFIG_RELOAD_AUTOMATIC=true
 - CONFIG_RELOAD_INTERVAL=300
 - DB_HOST=DATABASE-SERVER
 volumes:
 - devilbox-logstash:/var/lib/logstash
 depends_on:
 - elastic

 kibana:
 image: docker.elastic.co/kibana/kibana-oss:${ELK_SERVER:-6.6.1}
 hostname: kibana
 ports:
 - "${LOCAL_LISTEN_ADDR}${HOST_PORT_ELK_KIBANA:-5601}:5601"
 networks:
 app_net:
 ipv4_address: 172.16.238.242
 environment:
 - TZ=${TIMEZONE:-UTC}
 - ELASTICSEARCH_URL=http://elastic:9200
 depends_on:
 - elastic

volumes:
 devilbox-elastic:
 devilbox-logstash:

```

items.original_body:
## Describe the bug

Right now, MCTS does selfplay by playing random moves.
It should be doing a shallow search on every move instead.

items.original_body:
My previous question was closed - "The importer is looking for SF3Track2.bin and it does not exist. SF3Track2.bin is not the same as Shining Force III - Scenario 1 Track2.bin, now is it? From the messages, it is obvious that those file names need to match what is referenced in the cue sheet."

If you look at the first console screen I posted, you'll see I *did* edit the .cue file so the file names matched and received the same error. I was trying to demonstrate that the error happened in both instances.

![Screen Shot 2021-04-20 at 10 06 27 AM](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/82835504/115437683-557dcd00-a1c1-11eb-8944-d9681c3d2b23.png)

items.original_body:
With #46, this bit of code

https://github.com/wincowgerDEV/OpenSpecy/blob/3f6922d88f66de37a269dc5033fd1a6c486508e1/inst/shiny/server.R#L132-L139

changed from a generic way of sharing spectra to relying exclusively on Dropbox and bypassing the config.yml. Should we keep it this way?

I'd rather argue in favor of keeping the package functions the heart of the Shiny app and using them whenever possible. I agree that this is challenging when it comes to spectra sharing but I guess it's still possible if the `share_spec` function allows for (1) preparing files the way we would like them to receive and (2) provide interfaces to share those via e-mail (for package users) or dropbox (if hosted). In my opinion, this should not be too complicated.

If you agree, I could make the necessary changes with one of the next PRs.
items.mutated_title: changed from a generic way of sharing spectra to relying exclusively on Dropbox and ignoring the config.yml.
items.mutated_body:
With #46, this bit of code

https://github.com/wincowgerDEV/OpenSpecy/blob/3f6922d88f66de37a269dc5033fd1a6c486508e1/inst/shiny/server.R#L132-L139

changed from a generic way of sharing spectra to relying exclusively on Dropbox and bypassing the config.yml. Should we keep it this way?

I'd rather argue in favor of keeping the package functions the heart of the Shiny app and using them whenever possible. I agree that this is challenging when it comes to spectra sharing but I guess it's still possible if the `share_spec` function allows for (1) preparing files the way we would like them to receive and (2) provide interfaces to share those via e-mail (for package users) or dropbox (if hosted). In my opinion, this should not be too complicated.

If you agree, I could make the necessary changes with one of the next PRs.

items.original_title: CLI crashes when the config file is missing
items.original_body: Running the command without a config file raises an uncaught exception instead of showing a helpful error.
items.mutated_title: How do I install the CLI on Windows?
items.mutated_body: I am new to this project and would like to know the correct installation steps for Windows.
